rag_rules: Use only identifiers and types confirmed by the AADL model or visible-symbol set.
Keep port-based assumptions and guarantees in valid component type contracts; use implementation scope only for valid implementation-level bindings.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in a type-level contract unless they are exposed through contract-visible variables or outputs.
generic_rules: Encode numeric range constraints as boolean comparisons over a visible scalar symbol.
Introduce helper equations only when useful, and make the declared type match the expression type.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Write AGREE contracts inside annex agree {** ... **};
Comparison and range expressions evaluate to bool.
anti_patterns: Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE variables unless the model explicitly exposes them as typed ports or constants.
Do not convert Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data into numeric 0/1 checks or numeric ranges.
